perturbation.original_text: Referral Fees will be paid within thirty (30) days of the collection of receivables from the Registered Referral.
perturbation.changed_text: Referral Fees will be paid within sixty (60) days of the collection of receivables from the Registered Referral.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the payment deadline for Referral Fees. Section 6 originally states payment within 30 days, but this is modified to 60 days. This contradicts the payment terms outlined in Exhibit B.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Payment Terms: For a registered referral where the customer purchases directly from Company or another channel/contract vehicle other than Reseller, earned commissions are payable within thirty (30)days of when such amounts are collected by Company. For a Registered Opportunity where the customer purchases from Reseller, payment will be made by Reseller to Company within 5 days of receipt of payment (paid when paid). Expense Reimbursement: Reimbursement will be provided ONLY for expenses PRE-APPROVED in writing by Company. Marketing Expense: As may be agreed to by the parties in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated referral fees would be paid within 30 days, which aligns with the contradicted text stating commissions are payable within 30 days. The changed text alters this to 60 days, directly contradicting the contradicted text's 30-day payment term. This creates a clear inconsistency regarding payment deadlines, leading to uncertainty and a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Either Party may terminate this agreement for non-cause with a sixty (60) written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party may terminate this agreement for non-cause with a thirty (30) written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that either party can terminate with 60 days written notice. By changing this to 30 days, we create a conflict with Section 10, which implicitly refers back to this initial term regarding the termination process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Termination. a. Unless terminated earlier as provided herein, this Agreement shall have the Term set forth above (which commences on the Effective Date). b. This Agreement may be terminated by Reseller for cause immediately upon the occurrence of any of the following events: (i) if the use or sale of Company Products are enjoined due to any claim of infringement of any U.S. patent or copyright or of any misappropriation of trade secrets; (ii) if Company ceases to do business, or otherwise terminates its business operations; or (iii) if Company breaches any provision of this Agreement and fails to fully cure such breach within fifteen (15) business days of written notice from Reseller describing the breach. c. This Agreement may be terminated by Company for cause immediately upon the occurrence of any of the following events: (i) if Reseller ceases to do business, or otherwise terminates its business operations; or (ii) if Reseller breaches any provision of this Agreement and fails to fully cure such breach within fifteen (15) business days of written notice from Company describing the breach. d. Neither party shall incur any liability whatsoever for any damage, loss or expenses of any kind suffered or incurred by the other party arising from or incident to any termination of this Agreement, which complies with the terms of the Agreement, whether or not the terminating party is aware of any such damage, loss or expenses.
perturbation.justification: The original agreement allowed termination with 60 days' notice. The changed text reduces this to 30 days. Section 'd' of the 'Termination' section states that neither party is liable for termination that 'complies with the terms of the Agreement'. Because the notice period has been altered from 60 to 30 days, it directly contradicts and makes uncertain what the 'terms of the Agreement' are, creating a strong inconsistency regarding the legal requirements for termination and potential liability.

perturbation.original_text: Registered Opportunity: · "Reseller" will purchase directly from the "Company" at a 25% discount off the COMPANY-RESELLER AGREED UPONsale price.
perturbation.changed_text: Registered Opportunity: · "Reseller" will purchase directly from the "Company" at a 15% discount off the COMPANY-RESELLER AGREED UPONsale price.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation modifies the discount rate for Registered Opportunities from 25% to 15%. This directly contradicts the compensation terms specified in Exhibit B, creating an in-text contradiction regarding the financial benefits for the reseller.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RESELLER COMPENSATION Earned Commission: Reseller will be compensated for Registered Referrals and Registered Opportunities (as defined in Section 1g, 1i.) based on the following schedule: Registered Referral: · 25% of Net Revenue (as defined in Section 1e.) with a COMPANY-RESELLER AGREED UPON SALE PRICE in writing, the case of COMPANY's software products. The Company's Telecom Services will be agreed upon on a case-by-case basis in writing. Registered Opportunity: · "Reseller" will purchase directly from the "Company" at a 15% discount off the COMPANY-RESELLER AGREED UPONsale price. · If sale is made through SEWP, there will be an additional 4% discount off the sale price to cover the Prime contractusage fee (typically 7-10%) off the COMPANY-RESELLAR AGREED UPON SALE PRICE. Fulfillment Transaction Discount (as defined in Section 1j): · "Reseller" will purchase directly from the "Company" at a 7-10% discount off the sale price. Payment Terms: For a registered referral where the customer purchases directly from Company or another channel/contract vehicle other than Reseller, earned commissions are payable within thirty (30)days of when such amounts are collected by Company. For a Registered Opportunity where the customer purchases from Reseller, payment will be made by Reseller to Company within 5 days of receipt of payment (paid when paid). Expense Reimbursement: Reimbursement will be provided ONLY for expenses PRE-APPROVED in writing by Company. Marketing Expense: As may be agreed to by the parties in writing.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated a 25% discount. The changed text modifies this to a 15% discount. The contradicted text contains both a 25% discount and a 15% discount for the same 'Registered Opportunity'. This creates a direct contradiction because the reseller compensation for the same opportunity is defined differently within the same document, creating confusion and potential unenforceability.
